concerns.analysis: The HTML output is missing multiple meaningful images. The first page of the source document features a prominent photo collage and the 'NC STATE UNIVERSITY' logo, which are absent in the HTML (only a generic 'empty white rectangle' image appears). Furthermore, Figures 1, 2, and 9 are missing their `` tags and have been replaced entirely by text placeholders like '[Image: A bar graph...]'. Figures 7 and 11 have been improperly cropped or misidentified, capturing only page footers or blank b
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 1 contains a photo collage and university logo. Page 15 contains Figure 7, a bar/line chart titled 'Lake Gaston Hydrilla'. Page 10 contains bar charts for Figures 1 and 2.
concerns.output_quote: The first page images are missing. Figures 1, 2, and 9 are rendered as text (e.g., '[Image: A bar graph...]'). The image for Figure 7 has an alt text of 'The image displays the footer of a document page' and does not contain the chart.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (38): Lake Gaston Aquatic Vegetation Report 2023; 1 | P a g e; Lake Gaston Aquatic Vegetation Report 2023
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=38, pages_affected=19/20. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

concerns.description: Table caption coded as header row: 2 table(s) have a single spanning first row that is likely a caption/title
concerns.analysis: caption_as_header=2. Examples: p8: "LAKE GASTON AQUATIC PLANT COMMUNITY - 2023"; p9: "LAKE GASTON HYDRILLA TUBER BANK ESTIMATES". First row spans all columns — should be a <caption> element, not a header row.

The source document contains underlined text on the title page ('Prepared By:' and 'Submitted To:') to denote specific metadata sections. While the extraction preserved the italics, it completely lost the underline emphasis. Since underline has no markdown equivalent and was omitted from the JSON, this is a genuine loss of visual emphasis.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 2 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=2. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
